title: ulmus thomasii
intro: Ulmus thomasii, also known as Rock Elm, is a deciduous tree native to North America, belonging to the Elm family (Ulmaceae). The bark and inner bark of this tree have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. Ulmus thomasii has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive problems, and treating wounds due to its antimicrobial properties. The inner bark is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
